content.full_text:
SUPERSEDED
by: 1822{c)(4

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 1, 1992


Ronald W. Beals
Vice President Legal Counsel
Gannett Outdoor Co.
2327 Capitol Avenue
Sacramento, CA 95816


Re: Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Advice
Our File No. I-92-151

Dear Mr. Beals:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act.¹ Because you have not indicated the
identity of the candidate to be featured in your billboard we
treat your request as one for informal assistance pursuant to
Regulation 18329(c)(3).²

Your letter accurately summarizes my advice that a billboard
promoting the Congress of Racial Equality ("CORE") which features
a CORE official, who is also a candidate in a municipal election,
is not a contribution to the official, so long as the billboard is
not in or situated such that it would be directed to the voters in
the candidate's municipality. (See Dowd Advice Letter, No. A-86-
0, formerly No. A-86-054, copy enclosed.)


¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sections
18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-56

Page 2
Beals, Ronald W.


     If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel
                                        [signature]
                                   By:  Wayne P. Imberi
                                        Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure

sections.conclusion: Your letter accurately summarizes my advice that a billboard promoting the Congress of Racial Equality ("CORE") which features a CORE official, who is also a candidate in a municipal election, is not a contribution to the official, so long as the billboard is not in or situated such that it would be directed to the voters in the candidate's municipality.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a billboard promoting a non-profit organization (CORE) that features one of the organization's officials who is also a candidate in a municipal election constitutes a contribution to that candidate under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A billboard promoting CORE featuring one of its officials who is also a municipal election candidate is not a contribution to the candidate, provided the billboard is not directed at voters in the candidate's municipality.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is confirmation of informal telephone advice rather than a formal advice letter. No explicit QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers; conclusion reconstructed from body text. Marked 'SUPERSEDED' at top. Treats request as informal assistance under Regulation 18329(c)(3), which provides no immunity.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a billboard promoting a non-profit organization (CORE) that features one of the organization's officials who is also a candidate in a municipal election constitutes a contribution to that candidate under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: Your letter accurately summarizes my advice that a billboard promoting the Congress of Racial Equality ("CORE") which features a CORE official, who is also a candidate in a municipal election, is not a contribution to the official, so long as the billboard is not in or situated such that it would be directed to the voters in the candidate's municipality.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms telephone advice to Gannett Outdoor Co. regarding whether a billboard promoting CORE and featuring a CORE official who is a municipal election candidate constitutes a campaign contribution. The FPPC concluded it does not constitute a contribution if the billboard is not directed to voters in the candidate's municipality.
